engineGuide.h2: Peugeot Bipper Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot Bipper across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level 1.4 HDi unit powering early Bipper vans; reliable but prone to turbo oil feed issues at higher mileage.
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leak causing smoke and power loss (60k-90k miles)
Injector seat wear leading to hard starting
EGR valve clogging on short-journey use
entries.cta: Get quotes for 8HX Peugeot Bipper engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Updated 75hp tune of the DV4TD introduced with 2010 facelift; improved torque but retained timing belt design.
compatibleVariants: S, SE, Professional, Outdoor, Tepee (facelift models)
commonFailures: Injector failure causing black smoke and rough idle (70k-100k miles)
Turbo actuator sticking leading to limp mode
"Won't rev over 2000rpm" fault from MAF or EGR issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for 8HZ Peugeot Bipper engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Variant of DV4TD fitted to Outdoor and Tepee trims; identical internals to 8HX/8HZ with minor ECU mapping differences.
commonFailures: Turbo failure from oil starvation (timing belt debris)
Fuel pump wear causing hard cold starts

entries.title: related issues on later Euro 5 models
entries.cta: Get quotes for 8HS Peugeot Bipper engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: Fiat-derived 1.3 HDi unit introduced 2010; chain-driven, more refined but sensitive to oil quality and service intervals.
commonFailures: EGR cooler failure causing coolant loss and overheating
Timing chain tensioner wear at 80k+ miles
Injector seal leaks leading to diesel smell in cabin
entries.cta: Get quotes for FHY Peugeot Bipper engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: Higher-output 80hp version of F13DTE6; same architecture with revised turbo and ECU for improved response.
commonFailures: Turbo vane sticking causing intermittent power loss
Oil pump wear leading to low pressure warnings
Timing chain guide failure on neglected units
entries.cta: Get quotes for FHZ Peugeot Bipper engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Simple, robust 8-valve petrol engine; favoured for ULEZ compliance and lower repair complexity vs diesel options.
compatibleVariants: S, SE, Outdoor, Tepee (all petrol variants)
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup from infrequent servicing (40k+ miles)
Coil pack failure causing misfires and rough running
Timing belt failure (interference engine) if overdue
entries.cta: Get quotes for KFV Peugeot Bipper engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Base code for the 1.4 8v petrol family; widely used across PSA small vans with proven reliability when maintained.
compatibleVariants: S Petrol, SE Petrol, Tepee Petrol
commonFailures: Head gasket failure on overheated units
Water pump failure causing coolant loss
Throttle body carbon buildup affecting idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for TU3A Peugeot Bipper engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot Bipper Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Peugeot Bipper Turbo Failure - Why DV4TD Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Bipper 1.4 HDi S, SE, Professional, Outdoor (2008-2017, 8HX/8HZ/8HS DV4TD)
problems.rootCause: Oil starvation from clogged feed pipes or timing belt debris entering the turbo bearing housing, causing shaft wear and boost loss.

repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if vanes intact; may recur
12-18 months if oil system cleaned first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, 12m warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Bipper worth £1,500-£3,000, a turbo rebuild may suffice if caught early and oil system is clean. For high-mileage units or recurring issues, a rebuilt DV4TD engine with warranty offers better long-term value and minimises business downtime.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV4TD engine prices for your Peugeot Bipper

problems.h4: Peugeot Bipper Injector Failure - Hard Starting & Black Smoke
problems.rootCause: Injector nozzle wear or seat erosion allowing fuel leakage into cylinders, causing poor atomisation, black smoke and difficult cold starts.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term if other injectors aged similarly

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new injectors, 12m warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when multiple injectors failing

problems.recommendation: If only one injector is faulty and mileage is under 80k, replacement may be cost-effective. For units over 90k miles or with multiple injector codes, a rebuilt engine with fresh injectors and warranty is the smarter commercial decision.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Bipper injector failure repair or replacement

problems.group: Peugeot Bipper Won't Rev Over 2000 RPM
problems.h4: Peugeot Bipper Won't Rev Over 2000 RPM - Limp Mode Fault
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Bipper 1.4 HDi all variants (2008-2017, DV4TD codes)
problems.rootCause: MAF sensor contamination, EGR valve sticking, or turbo boost pressure sensor fault triggering ECU limp mode to protect the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean/replace MAF, EGR or boost sensor
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if fault is sensor-only, not mechanical

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Blank EGR valve, remap ECU (where legal)
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents recurrence but may affect MOT/emissions

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated sensors, 12m warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if fault stems from internal wear

problems.recommendation: Start with diagnostic testing to isolate the sensor. If the fault returns after sensor replacement, internal wear (turbo, EGR passages) is likely - a rebuilt engine with warranty prevents repeat downtime for commercial users.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot Bipper 2000rpm fault repair vs replacement costs

problems.h4: Peugeot Bipper Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Engine Damage
problems.rootCause: Timing belt snapping due to age, oil contamination or missed replacement; interference design causes valve/piston collision.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace belt, tensioner, water pump, seals
Replace bent valves, reface head, reset timing
Supply & fit rebuilt engine, 12m warranty, upgraded components

problems.recommendation: If the belt snapped and the engine won't turn over, assume catastrophic damage - replacement is the only reliable solution. For preventative maintenance, replace the belt at 10 years or 112,000 miles whichever comes first.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Bipper timing belt replacement or rebuilt engine

problems.h4: Peugeot Bipper Oil Leak - Turbo Feed or Sump Gasket
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Bipper 1.4 HDi all variants (2008-2017, DV4TD)
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed pipe seal degradation or sump gasket failure from vibration, causing oil loss and potential turbo starvation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo feed seal or sump gasket
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early before turbo damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned turbo if damaged by oil loss
Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded seals, 12m warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil loss caused internal wear

problems.recommendation: Address oil leaks immediately - even small losses can starve the turbo. If the turbo is already noisy or smoking, a rebuilt engine with warranty is more cost-effective than sequential repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot Bipper oil leak repair vs engine replacement prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot Bipper
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing turbo failure, injector issues, or the dreaded 2000rpm limp mode, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot Bipper specialists. Every rebuilt and reconditioned engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get your van back on the road without the main dealer premium.

Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot Bipper is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Peugeot Bipper 1.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.3 HDi F13DTE6 engine was introduced with the 2010 Bipper facelift, offering improved refinement and Euro 5 compliance over the older 1.4 HDi. This Fiat-derived chain-driven unit powers later Professional and Outdoor trims, with 75hp and 80hp outputs available.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Bipper 1.3L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot Bipper 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4 HDi DV4TD is the Bipper's most common diesel engine, fitted across all generations from 2008-2017. With outputs of 68hp and 75hp, it powers S, SE, Professional and Outdoor variants, but is known for turbo and injector-related failures at higher mileage.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Bipper 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot Bipper 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4 8v TU3A petrol engine offers a simpler, ULEZ-compliant alternative to diesel for urban commercial use. Fitted to S, SE and Tepee variants throughout production, it delivers 73-75hp with lower repair complexity but requires strict servicing to avoid sludge issues. Not sure which engine size your Peugeot Bipper needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot Bipper's 2008-2017 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Bipper launched with the proven 1.4 HDi DV4TD diesel and 1.4 TU3A petrol, establishing the platform for small commercial use.
keyChanges: Bipper van and Tepee passenger variants introduced
1.4 HDi 68hp (8HX) and 1.4 petrol 73hp (KFV) as standard engines
Basic S and SE trim levels available
knownFor: Simple, robust mechanicals ideal for urban commercial use
Early turbo oil feed issues on 1.4 HDi at higher mileage
Timing belt maintenance critical on both diesel and petrol

keyChanges: 1.3 HDi 75hp (FHY) added to Professional and Outdoor trims
1.4 HDi updated to 75hp (8HZ) with revised ECU mapping
knownFor: Improved refinement and Euro 5 compliance with 1.3 HDi

Injector failure becoming common on high-mileage 1.4 HDi

Final production year 2017 before model discontinuation

Strong residual demand for rebuilt engines due to commercial utility

faq.h2: Peugeot Bipper Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot Bipper engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot Bipper engines typically cost £795-£2,000 supply-only, depending on engine code and condition. Used units start from £450, while supply & fit services add £400-£750 labour.
keyPoints: 1.4 HDi DV4TD rebuilt: £895-£1,800 supply-only
1.4 Petrol TU3A rebuilt: £795-£1,600 supply-only
1.3 HDi F13DTE6 rebuilt: £995-£2,000 supply-only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£750
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Bipper engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot Bipper engine?
items.answer: Yes, if your Bipper is otherwise sound and valued at £1,500-£3,000. A rebuilt engine with warranty often costs less than vehicle replacement and restores commercial utility.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost typically 30-50% of van's market value
12-month warranty protects your investment
Supply & fit minimises business downtime
Retain familiar vehicle vs. buying unknown used van
items.cta: Get a tailored quote for your Peugeot Bipper engine replacement

items.question: What engines fit a Peugeot Bipper?
items.answer: The Bipper uses three main engine families: 1.4 HDi DV4TD (8HX/8HZ/8HS), 1.4 petrol TU3A (KFV), and 1.3 HDi F13DTE6 (FHY/FHZ). Exact code matching is essential for compatibility.
keyPoints: DV4TD codes: 8HX, 8HZ, 8HS, 8HR (1.4 diesel)
TU3A code: KFV (1.4 petrol)
F13DTE6 codes: FHY, FHZ (1.3 diesel)
Engines shared with Citroën Nemo and Fiat Fiorino
items.cta: Find compatible Peugeot Bipper engines by entering your reg

items.question: How long does Peugeot Bipper engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines can be delivered next-day UK-wide. Supply & fit services typically complete installation in 1-2 days at a local specialist, minimising commercial downtime.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery available on most rebuilt units
Supply & fit installation: 1-2 working days typical
ECU adaptation and road test included in fit service
Weekend collection/delivery options at select garages
items.cta: Find Peugeot Bipper engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot Bipper replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Some suppliers offer extended terms up to 24 months.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour at supplier's garage
Extended warranty options available on rebuilt/reman units
Always confirm exact terms before ordering
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Bipper engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a Peugeot Bipper engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible with workshop equipment, professional fitting is recommended for commercial users to ensure ECU adaptation, warranty validity and minimal downtime.
keyPoints: Requires engine hoist, diagnostic tools and torque wrench
ECU coding/adaptation needed post-installation
Professional fit preserves warranty coverage
Supply & fit service saves time and ensures compliance
items.cta: Find vetted Peugeot Bipper engine fitting specialists near you

items.question: How quickly can a Peugeot Bipper engine be delivered?
items.answer: Most rebuilt and used Peugeot Bipper engines are available for next-day UK delivery from our network of specialists, with same-day dispatch possible on in-stock units.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery standard on most rebuilt units
Same-day dispatch available on in-stock engines
Nationwide courier network covers all UK postcodes
Weekend delivery options at select locations
items.cta: Check Peugeot Bipper engine availability and delivery times

items.question: Should I choose a used or rebuilt Peugeot Bipper engine?
items.answer: Choose used for tight budgets and short-term needs; choose rebuilt for commercial reliability, warranty protection and addressing known failure points like turbo seals.
keyPoints: Used: £450-£1,400, 3-6 month warranty, variable mileage
Rebuilt: £795-£2,000, 12-month warranty, upgraded components
Rebuilt engines address common DV4TD/TU3A weaknesses
Commercial users benefit from rebuilt reliability and warranty
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Peugeot Bipper engine options

items.question: Where is the Peugeot Bipper engine number located?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the engine block near the gearbox flange on DV4TD units, or on the timing cover on TU3A petrol engines. Check your V5C logbook for confirmation.
keyPoints: DV4TD (1.4 HDi): code on block near gearbox flange
TU3A (1.4 petrol): code on timing cover or block
V5C logbook lists engine code in section V.5
DVLA lookup via registration confirms exact engine
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot Bipper engine code instantly via reg lookup

items.question: What are the most common Peugeot Bipper engine problems?
items.answer: Turbo failure, injector issues and the "won't rev over 2000rpm" fault dominate Bipper replacement enquiries, particularly on high-mileage 1.4 HDi DV4TD engines.
keyPoints: Turbo oil seal leak causing smoke/power loss (60k-90k miles)
Injector failure leading to hard starts/black smoke (70k-100k miles)
MAF/EGR faults triggering 2000rpm limp mode
Timing belt failure causes catastrophic damage if overdue
items.cta: Compare repair vs replacement costs for your Peugeot Bipper engine
